content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The amendment correctly identifies a real defense-in-depth gap: validate_classification_fields exists but is not wired into deserialization or the batch validate_classifications helper. Any caller using standard serde deserialization will silently accept cross-variant field contamination. Since the module is newly added and has no callers yet, this is the right time to close this gap before consumers depend on the unsafe API.

## Votes

- `fr-6-39e81076`: Accept
  The defect is real and confirmed by code inspection. FindingClassification at line 49 derives plain Deserialize, so serde silently drops fields from other variants (e.g., mapped_to_bead_id on a fix-now payload). The JSON-level guard validate_classification_fields (line 161) detects this but is unreachable from normal API usage: it's only called in tests (lines 1007-1089), not from validate_classifications (line 288) or any deserialization path. This means the careful field-validation logic is effectively dead code for consumers. The fix — either a custom Deserialize impl that calls validate_classification_fields, a checked constructor that combines both validations, or wiring the JSON check into validate_classifications — is straightforward and closes the gap before any callers adopt the current unsafe API. A regression test for the cross-variant contamination scenario through the normal deserialization path would further solidify the fix.
